name: Emma

profile.shopping_behaviour: Tracks purchases in spreadsheet by cost-per-wear. Shops COS, Arket, Vinted (filtered by material/origin), and thrifts linen for sewing. Ruthlessly returns items like Aritzia pieces that lack quality justification.
profile.personality: Methodical, uses systems like 3-word method and seasonal tracking. Frustrated by mid-tier brand price-to-quality gap. Experiences anxiety about 323-item closet despite donating 67 pieces last year.
profile.style_identity: Scandi minimalist seeking natural fibers (100% linen, Made in France). Analyzes seam quality and construction. Prefers Arket/COS level where fabric quality justifies cost; aspires to luxury but values substance over prestige.

preview_description: Fiona, 34, shops intentionally at COS and Arket while sourcing quality pieces on Vinted, tracking cost-per-wear in spreadsheets. She judges garments by fabric, construction, and origin rather than labels, reducing her 323-item closet to achieve 91% usage last year.

preview_attributes.key_brands: COS, Arket, Vinted, M&S, vintage French brands, independent makers
preview_attributes.style_identity: Scandi minimalist with natural fiber obsession
preview_attributes.shopping_triggers: Exceptional fabric quality, ethical manufacturing, proven cost-per-wear potential, pieces that solve identified wardrobe gaps
preview_attributes.pain_points: Mid-tier brands charging premium prices for fast fashion quality, emotional attachment to unworn clothes, marketing pressure to buy trendy pieces that don't suit her style
